rulings.Case Title: BETTY WILLIAMS, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 75, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Produce Bankruptcy Trust Settlement Releases Pursuant To This Courts Order And To Compel Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Autozone West, Inc.'s motion to compel plaintiffs to produce bankruptcy trust settlement releases pursuant to this Court's order and to compel further response to request for production of documents no. 27 is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiffs have 10 days to produce the trust releases and to serve a further response to the request for production. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Volkswagen Group Of America, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Oder To Require Plaintiffs To Reduce Their Expert Witness List And Provide Proper Designations Of Testimony
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s motion for protective order to require plaintiffs to reduce their expert witness list and provide proper designations of testimony: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
